title: Plesioneuron irayense
intro: Plesioneuron irayense, also known as Irayen or Tagalog, is a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Plesioneuron irayense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Plesioneuron irayense is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is often used to make teas, capsules, and extracts.
